activities_projects.title: Calculating Earth’s Rotation Speed at Different Latitudes
activities_projects.objective: Estimate linear speed of Earth’s rotation at equator and other latitudes
steps: Measure circumference of globe at equator and at tilted latitudes
Use proportion to estimate actual Earth circumference
Calculate linear speed: speed = circumference / 24 hours
Compare speed at equator vs higher latitudes
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands variation of rotational speed with latitude

activities_projects.title: Modeling Eclipses with Scale Angles
activities_projects.objective: Understand precise alignment during solar and lunar eclipses
steps: Place Moon at different distances between Sun and Earth for solar eclipse
Place Moon opposite Sun for lunar eclipse
Measure angles and shadow sizes
Explain partial vs total eclipses
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies geometry and scale modeling to eclipses
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct alignment, angle measurement, and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Calculating Day Length at Different Latitudes
activities_projects.objective: Estimate variation of day and night across Earth
steps: Tilt globe at 23.5° to simulate Earth’s axis
Shine torch to simulate Sun at solstices
Measure illuminated portion for different latitudes
Calculate approximate day length for equator, mid-latitude, and poles
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects Earth tilt and revolution to day length variation

activities_projects.title: Advanced Shadow Analysis with Sun Angles
activities_projects.objective: Relate shadow length and direction to solar altitude
steps: Measure shadow length at specific times of day
Calculate Sun’s angle of elevation: tan θ = stick height / shadow length
Compare calculated angle with actual solar position charts
Analyze seasonal variation
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies trigonometry to real-life observations of Sun position

activities_projects.title: Observing Moon Orbit and Calculating Synodic Month
steps: Record time between successive similar Moon phases over one month
Calculate average days between phases
Compare with actual synodic month (~29.5 days)
Analyze minor variations due to elliptical orbit
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects observations with Moon’s orbital period and motion

activities_projects.title: Simulating Seasons and Sun Angle Variation
activities_projects.objective: Model seasonal variation of sunlight intensity
steps: Tilt globe at 23.5°
Simulate solstice positions with torch
Measure Sun’s angle at different latitudes
Explain effect on temperature and day length
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Relates Earth tilt, Sun angle, and seasonal changes quantitatively

activities_projects.title: Modeling Umbra and Penumbra in Eclipses
steps: Create shadow of Moon on Earth (solar eclipse)
Identify umbra (total) and penumbra (partial) regions
Simulate lunar eclipse and observe Earth’s shadow on Moon
Draw scale diagram showing shadow regions
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands umbra and penumbra and their effects on eclipses

activities_projects.title: Comparing Earth Rotation and Revolution Speeds
activities_projects.objective: Analyze and calculate rotational vs orbital speed
steps: Calculate Earth rotation speed at equator (circumference / 24 hours)
Calculate Earth revolution speed (orbital circumference / 365.25 days)
Compare speeds and discuss implications for day-night vs year cycle
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands difference between rotation and revolution quantitatively

activities_projects.title: Critical Thinking: Eclipse Prediction
activities_projects.objective: Predict possibility of solar or lunar eclipse at given dates
materials: Calendar or software for Moon phases
steps: Check Moon phase for given date
Place Moon relative to Sun and Earth using model
Predict whether solar or lunar eclipse can occur
Explain reasoning using geometry
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies observation, geometry, and Moon phase knowledge to predict real events

activities_projects.title: Advanced Quiz on Earth-Moon-Sun System
steps: Prepare five calculation-based questions (shadows, angles, rotations, eclipses)
Prepare five reasoning/real-world application questions
Exchange with a friend and solve analytically
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Strengthens analytical, observational, and applied understanding
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of questions and correctness of answers (10 marks)
